_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building and presenting AI proof-of-concept demonstrations to executive audiences, communicating capability, limitations, and path to production.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-S-019 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-009 (DG, cosine: 0.409), OP-T-004 (OP, cosine: 0.409).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.422). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [action, courses, develop, potential] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, audiences, building, capability, communicating, concept] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.887, R=0.848). P exceeds R by 0.040 — the DCWF element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 18 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Develop potential courses of action." and "Skill in building and presenting AI proof-of-concept demonstrations to executive" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.418 ≈ STS 0.422 (gap 0.004). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.5%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [action, courses, develop, potential]. WIDAI terms: [ai, audiences, building, capability, communicating, concept].
